main.description: A shared objective the crew works toward (TASK-84). Published as the latest-value artifact goal.<goal-id> — a north-star plus its acceptance criteria. The goal's STATUS IS DERIVED from the criteria rollup (N of M met), not a stored field. Distinct from agent.status (what one agent is doing right now): a goal is a tracked OUTCOME many agents move. The goal.<id> artifact is the current value; goal.update messages on msg.topic.goals are the observable stream of transitions. Content is opaque to the bus; this is a convention, not enforcement — see ADR-0035.

northstar.description: The objective in one line — what 'done' looks like, e.g. 'Ship v0.5.0 with the goal primitive'.

stream.description: Optional grouping label so related goals read together in a UI (e.g. 'v0.5', 'hardening'). A free label, opaque to the bus.

criteria.description: The acceptance criteria that define the goal. The goal's status is DERIVED from these (how many are met of the total) — there is no separate stored state field. An empty list reads as not-started.

id.description: Criterion id, unique within the goal; the stable handle an artifact's relates[].crit points at.

text.description: The criterion in one line — a checkable outcome, e.g. 'goal + goal.update lexicons land on v0.5'.

status.description: One of: met (satisfied; invariant — has >=1 proof-kind artifact in relates) | in-progress (actively being worked) | waiting-on-you (soft wait on a person) | blocked (HARD blocked, needs help) | not-started. Who flips it is fuzzy and uncoded: a mechanically-testable criterion self-serves (an agent runs it, writes a proof artifact, flips it); an agent may infer a human-approved artifact means met; or the operator sets it directly.

owner.description: Optional agent accountable for this criterion (convenience label, not an assignment — signal, not manage).

by.description: Who last set it (convenience label; the bus-stamped author of the artifact write is authoritative).

verbs.description: The goals convention's verb signatures — the canonical interface contract every language's conv/goals library implements over its own SDK (ADR-0041: a convention is a library over the SDK, never a bus feature). Each verb names its domain input and the ordered primitive bus operations it emits; the recorded conformance vectors under protocol/conformance/vectors/goals pin that an implementation produces exactly these operations. Verb LOGIC is hand-written per language (concept, not codegen); only the record TYPES above are generated.

setCriterion.summary: Set one criterion's status on a goal: read goal.<goalId> (artifact.get), rewrite that criterion's status in place (every other field preserved), compare-and-set it back (artifact.update), then announce the transition on msg.topic.goals (message.publish goal.update). Idempotent: a criterion already at the target status is a no-op — no write, no announce. status=met is invariant — a criterion only reads met with >=1 proof-kind artifact in relates; setting met without proof is the caller's assertion, the read-side proof-filter is the single arbiter.

input.goalId: string — the goal-id; the artifact is goal.<goalId>
input.criterionId: string — the criterion id to set, unique within the goal
input.status: string — one of: met | in-progress | waiting-on-you | blocked | not-started
input.headline: string — short line describing the transition for the goal.update announcement
input.ref: string (optional) — artifact/PR/ticket that triggered the update

operations: artifact.update goal.<goalId> (the goal record with the criterion's status rewritten; expectedRev = the get's revision)
message.publish msg.topic.goals (a goal.update record: goal, crit, status, headline, ref)
